tool_call.name: retrieve_information
tool_call.args: {"prompt":"What is the construction-in-progress (CIP) balance and total property, plant and equipment balance disclosed in {{spacex_s1}}? Please provide the PP&E breakdown by category (servers, satellites, flight vehicles, data centers, etc.) and any forward-looking statements about future capital expenditures or AI infrastructure investment plans."}

parsed_args.prompt: What is the construction-in-progress (CIP) balance and total property, plant and equipment balance disclosed in {{spacex_s1}}? Please provide the PP&E breakdown by category (servers, satellites, flight vehicles, data centers, etc.) and any forward-looking statements about future capital expenditures or AI infrastructure investment plans.

tool_output.output:
# Construction-in-Progress and Total PP&E Balances

## Construction-in-Progress (CIP) Balance

| Period | CIP Balance |
|---|---|
| March 31, 2026 | **$14,045 million** |
| December 31, 2025 | **$4,604 million** |
| December 31, 2024 | **$3,007 million** |

CIP is described as "primarily comprised of ongoing construction and expansion of the facilities and equipment as well as AI infrastructure that has not yet been placed in service." The CIP balance nearly tripled from $4.6 billion at year-end 2025 to $14.0 billion by Q1 2026, reflecting the rapid acceleration of AI data center buildout.

## Total Property, Plant, and Equipment (Gross, Before Accumulated Depreciation)

| Period | Total PP&E (Gross) |
|---|---|
| March 31, 2026 | **$68,571 million** |
| December 31, 2025 | **$55,303 million** |
| December 31, 2024 | **$28,687 million** |

## Full PP&E Breakdown by Category

| Category | March 31, 2026 | December 31, 2025 | December 31, 2024 |
|---|---|---|---|
| Servers and networking equipment | $23,850 | $22,694 | $6,892 |
| Satellites | 12,893 | 11,949 | 7,591 |
| Machinery and equipment | 8,020 | 6,343 | 5,343 |
| Data center infrastructure | 2,965 | 2,960 | 224 |
| Launch sites | 2,479 | 2,404 | 2,121 |
| Land, buildings and improvements | 2,018 | 1,876 | 913 |
| Flight vehicle hardware | 1,459 | 1,689 | 1,577 |
| Leasehold improvements | 842 | 784 | 1,019 |
| Construction-in-progress | 14,045 | 4,604 | 3,007 |
| **Total PP&E (gross)** | **$68,571** | **$55,303** | **$28,687** |
| Less: Accumulated depreciation | (14,692) | (12,701) | (7,540) |
| **PP&E, net** | **$53,879** | **$42,602** | **$21,147** |

### Estimated Useful Lives (from Significant Accounting Policies)

| Classification | Estimated Useful Life |
|---|---|
| Servers and networking equipment | 5–6 years |
| Satellites | 3–5 years |
| Machinery and equipment | 3–10 years |
| Flight vehicle hardware | 5–25 flights |
| Data center infrastructure | 20–25 years |
| Launch sites | 7–20 years |
| Buildings and improvements | 30 years |
| Leasehold improvements | Shorter of 7–20 years or lease life |

---

## Capital Expenditures by Segment

| Segment | Q1 2026 | Q1 2025 | FY 2025 | FY 2024 | FY 2023 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Space | $1,052 | $759 | $3,832 | $2,032 | $1,497 |
| Connectivity | 1,332 | 814 | 4,178 | 3,498 | 2,455 |
| AI | 7,723 | 2,567 | 12,727 | 5,633 | 463 |
| **Total CapEx** | **$10,107** | **$4,140** | **$20,737** | **$11,163** | **$4,415** |

The AI segment's capital expenditures surged from $463 million in 2023 to $12.7 billion in 2025, and reached $7.7 billion in just the first quarter of 2026 alone—exceeding all of FY 2024.

---

## Forward-Looking Statements on Future Capital Expenditures and AI Infrastructure

The document contains several explicit forward-looking statements regarding future investment plans:

### 1. Substantial Increase in Capital Expenditures Expected
> "We have incurred significant capital expenditures and **expect to increase our capital expenditures substantially in the future** in connection with the design, development, and deployment of our satellite constellations, launch vehicles, ground stations, manufacturing facilities, and programs, including **Terafab, AI compute infrastructure, data centers, and other supporting infrastructure**."

### 2. AI-Specific Investment Plans
> "We have made and **intend to continue to make substantial capital expenditures to support the growth of our AI products**, including costs related to **obtaining third-party GPUs, manufacturing our own GPUs, and constructing, leasing, maintaining, enhancing, and expanding our data centers**."

### 3. Acceleration Option
> "We **may choose to increase or accelerate the pace of any of these investments at any time**, which could result in periods of reduced profitability or increased losses as we prioritize long-term growth over near-term financial performance."

### 4. Starship-Driven Cost Reduction Enabling Orbital AI
> "We believe that Starship can eventually **reduce the cost to reach orbit by 99% or more** relative to the historical average launch cost per kilogram according to NASA of $18,500, establishing a **scalable path to creating the infrastructure of the future, such as orbital AI compute**."

### 5. Orbital AI Compute Satellite Deployment
> The company references "potential deployment of our **orbital AI compute satellites as early as 2028**," including the ability to "successfully develop, scale, and commercialize such technologies, which are subject to significant technical complexity, capital requirements, new innovations and regulatory approvals."

### 6. IPO Proceeds Allocation
> "We intend to use the net proceeds from this offering to fund our growth strategy, including the **expansion of our AI compute infrastructure, enhancements to our launch infrastructure and launch vehicles, increases in the scale and capacity of our satellite constellations**, and any remaining amounts for general corporate purposes."

### 7. Starship Capital Requirements
> "The development, testing, and deployment of Starship in accordance with our anticipated schedule, as well as our pursuit of orbital AI, other space-related services, and lunar and interplanetary missions, **will require the investment of significant additional capital resources**."

---

## Additional Context

- **Capitalized interest**: $169 million was capitalized into CIP for the year ended December 31, 2025 (related to AI infrastructure data centers), and $7 million for the three months ended March 31, 2026. No interest was capitalized in 2024 or 2023.
- **Depreciation expense**: $5,915 million (FY 2025), $2,977 million (FY 2024), and $2,329 million (Q1 2026).
- **Impairment charges**: $38 million in FY 2025 (damaged flight vehicle in Space segment; abandoned production line and damaged satellite hardware in Connectivity segment) and $24 million in Q1 2025 (damaged flight vehicles in Space segment). Additional restructuring-related impairments of $36 million (2024) and $54 million (2023) related to leasehold improvements and office equipment in the AI segment.
- **Related-party PP&E additions**: $506 million (2025) and $191 million (2024) of Megapack products purchased from Tesla, plus $131 million of Cybertrucks, all recorded in PP&E.

parsed_args.prompt: What does {{spacex_s1}} disclose about Colossus supercomputer, AI data center expansion plans, GPU purchases, and future AI infrastructure investment commitments? Please include any forward-looking statements about AI capex, GPU deployment, or data center buildout plans.

tool_output.output:
Based on the provided S-1 preamble excerpts, the company discloses a comprehensive, highly vertically integrated strategy for its AI infrastructure, centering on the COLOSSUS supercomputer, massive capital expenditures, and future expansion into orbital AI. 

Here is a detailed breakdown of the disclosures regarding the Colossus supercomputer, data center expansion, GPU purchases, and future investment commitments:

### 1. The COLOSSUS Supercomputer and Current GPU Deployment
The company highlights its existing gigawatt-scale AI training clusters as a primary competitive advantage, emphasizing rapid deployment and cost efficiency:
*   **Current Capacity:** COLOSSUS and COLOSSUS II collectively provide approximately **1.0 gigawatt of compute power**, with additional power capacity available for operations. COLOSSUS II is located in Memphis, Tennessee.
*   **Rapid Deployment:** The first cluster of COLOSSUS was brought online in **122 days** by repurposing an existing factory. The first cluster of COLOSSUS II was deployed even faster, in **91 days**. (The company notes the industry benchmark for bringing a 100MW greenfield data center online is approximately two years).
*   **GPU Deployment Milestones:** 
    *   **COLOSSUS:** First cluster deployed approximately 100,000 H100 processors (~130 megawatts).
    *   **COLOSSUS II:** First cluster deployed approximately 110,000 GB200 processors (~210 megawatts) in 91 days. The second cluster added 110,000 GB300 processors and 220 megawatts in just 64 days. The company states COLOSSUS II was one of the world's first data centers to deploy GB200s and GB300s at significant scale.

### 2. AI Data Center Expansion and Buildout Plans
The company outlines aggressive terrestrial expansion plans for COLOSSUS II, alongside a visionary long-term plan for orbital data centers:
*   **COLOSSUS II Expansion:** The next phase of expansion at COLOSSUS II is designed to train the next-generation Grok 5 AI model. Once fully operational, this phase will bring online **at least 220,000 additional GB300 processors** and represent an **additional 400+ megawatts of compute capacity**.
*   **Power and Cooling Infrastructure:** COLOSSUS II is capable of operating entirely off a self-built, behind-the-meter gigawatt-scale natural gas power plant, supplemented by the world’s largest Megapack (battery storage) deployment. The facilities use brownfield retrofits, advanced direct-to-chip liquid cooling, high-density rack layouts, and advanced water reclamation. The company pledges to cover the costs of all new power delivery infrastructure upgrades so expenses are not passed on to local households.
*   **Orbital AI Compute (Forward-Looking):** The company plans to eventually move power-intensive AI workloads into orbit to leverage near-constant solar energy, bypassing terrestrial energy constraints. They believe their Starship launch vehicle will enable the deployment and operation of orbital data centers that could eventually achieve lower costs than terrestrial data centers.

### 3. GPU Purchases and Chip Manufacturing (Terafab)
To secure the massive compute required and alleviate future chip shortages, the company is vertically integrating into hardware manufacturing:
*   **Third-Party GPUs:** The company incurs substantial costs related to obtaining third-party GPUs.
*   **Terafab Initiative:** Together with Tesla (and Intel, which joined in April 2026), the company is building a manufacturing facility capable of producing **1 terawatt per year of compute hardware**. This "shovels-to-tokens" approach extends their vertical integration into chip design and manufacturing to optimize performance and reduce overall compute costs.

### 4. AI Infrastructure Investment Commitments & Capex (Forward-Looking)
The S-1 details massive financial commitments and warns that prioritizing these long-term investments may impact near-term profitability:
*   **Historical Capex Surge:** AI capital expenditures increased from $463 million in 2023 to $5.6 billion in 2024, and surged to **$12.7 billion in 2025**. These investments were primarily driven by the rapid expansion of terrestrial data centers.
*   **Future Capex Expectations:** The company expects to *increase capital expenditures substantially* in the future for the design, development, and deployment of satellite constellations, launch vehicles, Terafab, AI compute infrastructure, and data centers. This includes manufacturing their own GPUs and constructing, leasing, maintaining, and expanding data centers.
*   **Recent Financial Commitments:**
    *   An **$8.5 billion deferred services fee** under a compute agreement (payable in cash or Class A common stock).
    *   A **five-year equipment lease agreement with Valor** for AI infrastructure hardware ("Valor transaction III") for total undiscounted lease payments of **$6.58 billion**.
    *   An asset purchase agreement to buy mobile gas turbines and related packages for approximately **$2 billion** to help provide power to the data centers.
    *   A cloud services agreement with Anthropic PBC for access to compute capacity, with monthly fees ramping through May 2029.
*   **Near-Term Profitability Warning:** The company explicitly states it may choose to increase or accelerate the pace of these investments at any time, which "could result in periods of reduced profitability or increased losses as we prioritize long-term growth over near-term financial performance."
